story.iWant: clear error messages when something goes wrong
story.soThat: I know what happened and how to fix it

tasks.task: Create error-handler.js module for centralized error handling
Implement ERROR_MESSAGES library with all error types (10+ errors)
Implement showError() function with error UI rendering
Create error panel UI component (header, body, details, actions)
Implement recovery action buttons (retry, reset, continue, help)
Add error logging to console with full context
Integrate with all existing stories (wrap operations in try-catch)
Add event listeners for errorRetry and errorReset
Add CSS styling for error panel (red theme, fixed position)
Add HTML container for error display
Test all error scenarios (10 error types)
User test error messages with non-technical users

criterion.title: Centralized Error Display Component
criterion.requirement: Create single error display component for all error types
Location: Below page header, above main content (always visible, fixed position)
Style: Red background, white text, icon, dismissible
Contains: Error icon (⚠️), user-friendly message, technical details (collapsible), action buttons
criterion.test: Trigger any error, verify error panel appears at top, red background, clear message, action buttons visible

criterion.title: Comprehensive Error Message Library
criterion.requirement: Define user-friendly messages for all error types: WASM loading, invalid file, file too large, file read, format detection, parameter extraction, conversion, download, browser compatibility, network errors
Error format: User-friendly message, What went wrong (simple), What to try (actionable), Technical details (collapsible)
No technical jargon in user-facing messages
All messages include actionable next step
criterion.test: Review all 10+ error messages, verify no jargon, all have actionable steps, technical details available

criterion.title: Error Recovery Actions
criterion.requirement: Try Again: Re-attempt last action (dispatch errorRetry event)
Reset: Clear all data, return to default state (dispatch errorReset event)
Help: Link to troubleshooting guide/FAQ (open in new tab)
Dismiss: Close error message but keep data/state (hide error panel)
criterion.test: Trigger conversion error → Try Again (re-attempts), Reset (clears UI), Help (opens FAQ), Dismiss (hides error)

criterion.title: Error Logging (Console + Optional Telemetry)
criterion.requirement: Log all errors to console with context: error type, technical + user message, timestamp, user action, file metadata
Never log sensitive data (file contents, user info)
Optional telemetry placeholder (not implemented in MVP)
criterion.test: Trigger any error, open DevTools, verify full context logged, no sensitive data, timestamp and action present

criterion.title: Error Boundaries (Prevent Full UI Crash)
criterion.requirement: Wrap critical components in error boundaries
If component crashes, show fallback UI (not white screen)
Fallback: "Something went wrong", "Please refresh the page", "If problem persists, contact support"
Log component crash to console
criterion.test: Simulate component crash, verify fallback UI displays, error logged, refresh recovers

criterion.title: Browser Compatibility Error Handling
criterion.requirement: Detect unsupported browsers (IE11, old Chrome <90)
Show message: "Recipe requires a modern browser. Please upgrade to Chrome, Firefox, or Safari."
Detect missing APIs: WebAssembly, FileReader, Blob
Show specific error for missing API
criterion.test: Test in IE11 or simulate with DevTools, verify "Unsupported browser" message, test with WASM disabled, verify specific error

criterion.title: Network Error Handling (WASM Loading)
criterion.requirement: If WASM fails to load: show "Unable to load converter. Please check your internet connection."
Provide retry button
Log error to console
Handle slow loading (>5s): show "Loading converter... (this may take a moment)"
criterion.test: Simulate network error (DevTools offline), verify error message, retry button works, re-enable network → retry succeeds

criterion.title: User Testing (Error Message Clarity)
criterion.requirement: Test error messages with non-technical users (photographers)
Verify messages understandable without technical knowledge
Verify action buttons are clear
Iterate based on feedback
criterion.test: Show error messages to 3 non-technical users, ask "What went wrong?" and "What would you do?", iterate if confused

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: Simple story (1-2 days). Deliverables: Error display component, error message mapping (technical → user-friendly), retry/reset actions. User-friendly messages are contextual and actionable. Error Categories: WASM load, invalid file, conversion, download, browser compatibility.

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: Inline error messages + console logging. Alerts are intrusive and block UI. Console logs help debugging but users don't see them. Inline messages user-friendly and contextual. Error Categories: WASM Load Failure (red banner), Invalid File (red banner), Conversion Error (red banner with details).

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: Users need clear explanation of what went wrong (not technical jargon), actionable next steps (retry? upload different file?), confidence that data is safe. Bad error: "TypeError: Cannot read property 'length' of undefined". Good error: "Unable to read file. Please try uploading again."

artifact.title: Architecture
artifact.snippet: Wrapped errors with custom ConversionError type (stdlib errors). Type-safe error checking, format-specific context, no dependencies. Error messages show user-friendly text to users, technical details to console.

artifact.title: Story 2-6: WASM Conversion Execution
artifact.snippet: Error message mapping: "NP3 magic bytes invalid" → "File appears corrupted or not a valid NP3 preset." "XMP parse error" → "Unable to parse XMP file. File may be corrupted." User-friendly errors only, technical to console, don't crash UI, error is recoverable.

artifact.title: Story 2-6: WASM Conversion Execution
artifact.snippet: Principle: Show user-friendly errors to users, technical errors to console. Example: Technical error (for developers): "NP3 parse error: invalid magic bytes at offset 0x00". User-friendly error (for photographers): "File appears corrupted or not a valid NP3 preset." Why? Users don't care about "magic bytes" or "offset 0x00".

artifact.title: Story 2-7: File Download Trigger
artifact.snippet: If download fails (browser blocks, no disk space): Show error "Download failed. Please check your browser settings and try again." Log technical error to console. Keep download button enabled (allow retry). Handle browser popup blockers.

artifact.title: Story 2-2: File Upload Handling
artifact.snippet: Invalid file type (not .np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate): Show error. File too large (>10MB): Show error. File read error (browser security, corrupted file): Show error with retry option.

artifact.reason: WASM loading logic. Can fail with network error or browser incompatibility. Needs error handling integration for "wasm-load-failed" error type.
File upload handling from Story 2-2. Can fail with "invalid-file-type", "file-too-large", "file-read-error". Needs error handling integration.
Format detection from Story 2-3. Can fail with "format-detection-failed". Needs error handling integration.
Parameter extraction from Story 2-4. Can fail with "parameter-extraction-failed". Needs error handling integration (non-blocking, allow continue).
Conversion logic from Story 2-6. Can fail with "conversion-failed" plus ConversionError with user-friendly messages. Primary error handling integration point.
Download logic from Story 2-7. Can fail with "download-failed". Needs error handling integration.
Central integration point. All event listeners need try-catch wrapping with error handler integration. Handles errorRetry and errorReset events.
Error container element (to be added). Fixed position at top of page, initially hidden, populated by error-handler.js.
Error panel styling. Red theme (#fed7d7 background, #742a2a text), fixed position, dismissible, collapsible details.

javascript.package: createElement, innerHTML for dynamic error UI
errorRetry, errorReset events for recovery actions
console.error() for error logging with context

browser.requirement: Required for errorRetry and errorReset events
Required for dynamic error UI rendering
Required for safe HTML escaping (XSS prevention)

story-dependencies.story: Provides file read errors, file too large errors
Provides conversion errors with ConversionError type and user-friendly messages
Provides WASM loading errors, browser compatibility errors

constraints.constraint: Cross-cutting concern - integrates with all Stories 2-1 through 2-7
Centralized error handling - single error-handler.js module, single ERROR_MESSAGES library
Event-driven recovery - dispatch errorRetry and errorReset events, don't call functions directly
Error panel fixed position at top of page (z-index: 1000)
Error panel always visible (not scrolled off screen)
User-friendly messages only - no technical jargon, stack traces, or error codes in UI
Technical details collapsible - hidden by default, developers can expand
All messages actionable - include "What to try" guidance
XSS prevention - use textContent for user-generated content, never innerHTML for error messages
Never log sensitive data - no file contents, no user information, no API keys
All errors to console with full context (type, message, timestamp, action, metadata)
Non-destructive retry - retry action doesn't lose user data or state
Reset is destructive - clears all state, returns to initial UI (page reload acceptable)
Browser detection before WASM loading - fail fast with clear message
Detect missing APIs (WebAssembly, FileReader, Blob) and show specific errors

interface.name: showError()
interface.description: Main error display function. Looks up error type in ERROR_MESSAGES, logs to console, renders error UI. Called by all modules when errors occur.

interface.name: hideError()
interface.description: Hides error panel. Called by dismiss button or when user takes recovery action.

interface.name: checkBrowserCompatibility()
interface.description: Checks for required browser features (WebAssembly, FileReader, Blob). Returns false and shows error if unsupported. Called on app initialization.

interface.name: errorRetry event
interface.description: Dispatched when user clicks "Try Again" button. Listened to by main.js to re-attempt last operation.

interface.name: errorReset event
interface.description: Dispatched when user clicks "Reset" button. Listened to by main.js to clear all state (typically triggers page reload).

interface.name: ERROR_MESSAGES
interface.signature: ERROR_MESSAGES: {[errorType: string]: {title, message, reason, action, recovery}}
interface.description: Centralized error message library. Maps error type keys to user-friendly message objects. Contains 10+ error types for all failure scenarios.

interface.name: ConversionError
interface.description: Custom error from Story 2-6. Contains userMessage property with user-friendly error text. Used for conversion failures.

standards.standard: Manual testing of all 10+ error scenarios (each error type triggered and verified)
Console inspection for error logging (verify full context, no sensitive data)
Visual verification of error panel (appearance, styling, positioning)
Recovery action testing (retry, reset, help, dismiss all functional)
Browser compatibility testing (IE11 or simulation, old Chrome, missing APIs)
User testing with non-technical users (3+ photographers, verify message clarity)
XSS security testing (verify user-generated content properly escaped)
Error boundary testing (simulate component crashes, verify fallback UI)

locations.location: web/static/error-handler.js (unit tests can be added inline or separate test file)
Browser console (verify error logging, check for sensitive data leaks)
DevTools Elements panel (inspect error panel DOM, verify XSS prevention)
All existing story modules (integration testing - trigger errors in each module)

ideas.idea: Test centralized error display: Trigger any error, verify panel appears at top, fixed position, red theme
Test all error types: Systematically trigger each of 10+ error types, verify correct message displayed, technical details available
Test recovery actions: Retry (re-attempts operation), Reset (clears UI/reloads), Help (opens FAQ), Dismiss (hides panel)
Test error logging: Trigger errors, verify console logs include timestamp, error type, user action, file metadata, no sensitive data
Test error boundaries: Simulate component crash (throw error in render), verify fallback UI, page refresh recovers
Test browser compatibility: Test in IE11 or simulate, verify "Unsupported browser" error, test with WebAssembly disabled
Test network errors: DevTools offline mode, load page, verify "Unable to load converter" error, retry works when online
User test error messages: Show 3 non-technical users error messages, ask comprehension questions, iterate based on feedback
Test WASM load failure: Block recipe.wasm in DevTools Network tab, verify error shown with retry option
Test invalid file type: Upload .jpg file, verify "Invalid File Type" error with Reset and Help actions
Test file too large: Create 15MB file, upload, verify "File Too Large" error with explanation
Test format detection failure: Upload file with random bytes, verify "Unknown Format" error
Test parameter extraction failure: Upload corrupted XMP, verify "Unable to Read Parameters" with "Continue Anyway" option
Test conversion failure: Upload corrupted NP3 (invalid magic bytes), convert, verify "Conversion Failed" error
Test download failure: Block downloads in browser settings, convert file, attempt download, verify "Download Failed" error
Test technical details toggle: Click "Show Technical Details", verify expands, click "Hide", verify collapses
Test XSS prevention: Inject HTML in filename or error message, verify properly escaped (no script execution)
Test error panel dismiss: Click × button, verify panel hides, state preserved, can continue working
Test multiple errors: Trigger error, dismiss, trigger different error, verify new error shown (old one cleared)
